paper_title: Predicting marsh vulnerability to sea-level rise using Holocene relative sea-level data

methods.method_name: Great British Holocene Relative Sea-Level Database Compilation
methods.description: Compilation of a database of index points from Holocene RSL data for Great Britain, derived from stratigraphic sequences that record tidal marsh retreat and advance.

steps.description: The Great British Holocene RSL database is derived from 54 regions based on availability of data and distance from the center of the British-Irish ice sheet.
steps.searchable_summary: compile RSL database from 54 regions in Great Britain

steps.description: The database includes over 80 fields of information for each index point, with a subset relevant to determine tidal marsh vulnerability.
steps.searchable_summary: process database fields to determine tidal marsh vulnerability

methods.description: Use of a glacial isostatic adjustment (GIA) model to determine the rates of RSLR for each index point of the database.

steps.description: Employ a GIA model to determine the rates of RSLR for each index point of the database.
steps.searchable_summary: apply GIA model to calculate RSLR rates

steps.description: Use linear interpolation to calculate the RSL at the median age of each sea-level index point.
steps.searchable_summary: perform linear interpolation for RSL calculation

methods.description: Estimation of the probability of a positive tendency conditional upon rates of RSLR using a statistical model.

steps.description: Convert the tendency data into a binary response variable and treat them as having a Bernoulli distribution.
steps.searchable_summary: convert tendency data to binary response variable

steps.description: Model the probabilities parameterizing the distribution using a flexible cubic penalized B-spline function.
steps.searchable_summary: model probabilities with cubic penalized B-spline

steps.description: Fit the model in a Bayesian framework and obtain posterior samples using a Markov chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) algorithm.
steps.searchable_summary: fit Bayesian model and obtain MCMC samples

methods.description: Generation of sea-level rise projections using Monte Carlo samples from the joint probability distribution of different contributing factors.

steps.description: Generate sea-level projections for tide-gauge locations using 10,000 Monte Carlo samples from the joint probability distribution of different contributing factors.
steps.searchable_summary: generate sea-level projections with Monte Carlo samples

steps.description: Determine the probability of a positive tendency for each Monte Carlo sample at each point in time.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze probability of positive tendency from Monte Carlo samples
